creator: ports.maintainer@evilphi.com
text:
(In reply to Michael Osipov from comment #7)

I think we're miscommunicating here.  My point in comment 2 was that FreeBSD doesn't have tools in base that can update the root store (meaning fetch new certs, process them into sanitized, meaningfully-named, individual PEM files, then invoke certctl to create the subject hash symlinks in /etc/ssl/certs).

You replied saying that point was wrong, but your later comments show that you had to write your own tools to do just that, in contradiction of your claim my point was wrong.  To me, it seems like we're in violent agreement somehow.

I'm also not sure why you're bringing any of this up, because this PR is only about moving ca-root-nss.crt out of the default TRUSTPATH for certctl so that the default behaviour of the in-base tool (certctl) and port (security/ca_root_nss) are no longer in conflict.  It's literally just to clean up the existing behaviour so there's less hurdles for the kinds of tools you and I have written to provide what FreeBSD hasn't.
